mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate crossing above 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition yields negative mean forward returns, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over a 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during tightening periods, with a negative Rank IC observed in > 50% of rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The coexistence of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses real corporate earnings and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses above 310 while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when both thresholds are breached, or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and wage growth, which expands consumer purchasing power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, with a mean forward return indistinguishable from zero across all historical regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth crossing above a 2.0% annualized pace confirms macroeconomic expansion and supports corporate earnings revisions, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and contraction in the unemployment rate signals a synchronized labor and output recovery, boosting equity risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate model over a 3-year rolling window.
